authors_text: Haruki Nishimura,Jean Mercat,Blake Wulfe,Rowan McAllister,Adrien Gaidon
title: RAP: Risk-Aware Prediction for Robust Planning
title_zh: RAP：稳健规划的风险感知预测
comment: 22 pages, 14 figures, 3 tables. First two authors contributed equally. Conference on Robot Learning (CoRL) 2022 (oral)
comments: 22 pages, 14 figures, 3 tables. First two authors contributed equally. Conference on Robot Learning (CoRL) 2022 (oral)
abstract: Robust planning in interactive scenarios requires predicting the uncertain future to make risk-aware decisions. Unfortunately, due to long-tail safety-critical events, the risk is often under-estimated by finite-sampling approximations of probabilistic motion forecasts. This can lead to overconfident and unsafe robot behavior, even with robust planners. Instead of assuming full prediction coverage that robust planners require, we propose to make prediction itself risk-aware. We introduce a new prediction objective to learn a risk-biased distribution over trajectories, so that risk evaluation simplifies to an expected cost estimation under this biased distribution. This reduces the sample complexity of the risk estimation during online planning, which is needed for safe real-time performance. Evaluation results in a didactic simulation environment and on a real-world dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach. The code and a demo are available.
summary: Robust planning in interactive scenarios requires predicting the uncertain future to make risk-aware decisions. Unfortunately, due to long-tail safety-critical events, the risk is often under-estimated by finite-sampling approximations of probabilistic motion forecasts. This can lead to overconfident and unsafe robot behavior, even with robust planners. Instead of assuming full prediction coverage that robust planners require, we propose to make prediction itself risk-aware. We introduce a new prediction objective to learn a risk-biased distribution over trajectories, so that risk evaluation simplifies to an expected cost estimation under this biased distribution. This reduces the sample complexity of the risk estimation during online planning, which is needed for safe real-time performance. Evaluation results in a didactic simulation environment and on a real-world dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach. The code and a demo are available.
abstract_zh: 交互式场景中的稳健规划需要预测不确定的未来，以做出具有风险意识的决策。不幸的是，由于长尾安全关键事件，概率运动预测的有限采样近似常常低估风险。即使有强大的规划者，这也可能导致过度自信和不安全的机器人行为。我们建议让预测本身具有风险意识，而不是假设稳健的规划者所要求的全面预测覆盖范围。我们引入了一个新的预测目标来学习轨迹上的风险偏向分布，以便风险评估简化为该偏向分布下的预期成本估计。这降低了在线规划期间风险估计的样本复杂性，这是安全实时性能所必需的。教学模拟环境和现实世界数据集的评估结果证明了我们方法的有效性。代码和演示可用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种风险感知的预测方法（RAP），通过学习轨迹的风险偏差分布来改进鲁棒规划中的风险评估。
AI.motivation: 现有基于有限采样的概率运动预测方法在长尾安全关键事件中容易低估风险，导致鲁棒规划器生成过度自信且不安全的机器人行为。
AI.method: 引入风险偏差分布学习目标，将轨迹预测转化为风险评估的期望成本估计，降低在线规划的样本复杂度。
AI.result: 在教学模拟环境和真实世界数据集上验证了方法的有效性，代码和演示已公开。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过风险感知预测提升了规划的安全性与实时性，为复杂交互场景提供了更可靠的决策框架。

search_text: RAP: Risk-Aware Prediction for Robust Planning RAP：稳健规划的风险感知预测 Robust planning in interactive scenarios requires predicting the uncertain future to make risk-aware decisions. Unfortunately, due to long-tail safety-critical events, the risk is often under-estimated by finite-sampling approximations of probabilistic motion forecasts. This can lead to overconfident and unsafe robot behavior, even with robust planners. Instead of assuming full prediction coverage that robust planners require, we propose to make prediction itself risk-aware. We introduce a new prediction objective to learn a risk-biased distribution over trajectories, so that risk evaluation simplifies to an expected cost estimation under this biased distribution. This reduces the sample complexity of the risk estimation during online planning, which is needed for safe real-time performance. Evaluation results in a didactic simulation environment and on a real-world dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach. The code and a demo are available. 交互式场景中的稳健规划需要预测不确定的未来，以做出具有风险意识的决策。不幸的是，由于长尾安全关键事件，概率运动预测的有限采样近似常常低估风险。即使有强大的规划者，这也可能导致过度自信和不安全的机器人行为。我们建议让预测本身具有风险意识，而不是假设稳健的规划者所要求的全面预测覆盖范围。我们引入了一个新的预测目标来学习轨迹上的风险偏向分布，以便风险评估简化为该偏向分布下的预期成本估计。这降低了在线规划期间风险估计的样本复杂性，这是安全实时性能所必需的。教学模拟环境和现实世界数据集的评估结果证明了我们方法的有效性。代码和演示可用。 本文提出一种风险感知的预测方法（RAP），通过学习轨迹的风险偏差分布来改进鲁棒规划中的风险评估。 Haruki Nishimura,Jean Mercat,Blake Wulfe,Rowan McAllister,Adrien Gaidon cs.LG,cs.RO

authors_text: Xiaoying Wei,Xiaofu Jin,Mingming Fan
title: Communication in Immersive Social Virtual Reality: A Systematic Review of 10 Years' Studies
title_zh: 沉浸式社交虚拟现实中的通信：十年研究的系统回顾
comment: Chinese CHI '22: The Tenth International Symposium of Chinese CHI (Chinese CHI 2022)
comments: Chinese CHI '22: The Tenth International Symposium of Chinese CHI (Chinese CHI 2022)
abstract: As virtual reality (VR) technologies have improved in the past decade, more research has investigated how they could support more effective communication in various contexts to improve collaboration and social connectedness. However, there was no literature to summarize the uniqueness VR provided and put forward guidance for designing social VR applications for better communication. To understand how VR has been designed and used to facilitate communication in different contexts, we conducted a systematic review of the studies investigating communication in social VR in the past ten years by following the PRISMA guidelines. We highlight current practices and challenges and identify research opportunities to improve the design of social VR to better support communication and make social VR more accessible.
summary: As virtual reality (VR) technologies have improved in the past decade, more research has investigated how they could support more effective communication in various contexts to improve collaboration and social connectedness. However, there was no literature to summarize the uniqueness VR provided and put forward guidance for designing social VR applications for better communication. To understand how VR has been designed and used to facilitate communication in different contexts, we conducted a systematic review of the studies investigating communication in social VR in the past ten years by following the PRISMA guidelines. We highlight current practices and challenges and identify research opportunities to improve the design of social VR to better support communication and make social VR more accessible.
abstract_zh: 随着虚拟现实 (VR) 技术在过去十年中不断进步，越来越多的研究调查了虚拟现实 (VR) 如何在各种环境下支持更有效的沟通，以改善协作和社交联系。然而，没有文献总结 VR 所提供的独特性，并为设计社交 VR 应用程序以实现更好的沟通提供指导。为了了解如何设计和使用 VR 来促进不同环境中的沟通，我们按照 PRISMA 指南对过去十年来调查社交 VR 中的沟通的研究进行了系统回顾。我们重点介绍当前的实践和挑战，并确定改进社交 VR 设计的研究机会，以更好地支持沟通并使社交 VR 更易于使用。

AI.tldr: 本文系统综述了过去十年关于社交虚拟现实（VR）中沟通的研究，旨在总结VR在促进沟通中的独特性并提出设计指导。
AI.motivation: 随着VR技术的发展，现有研究缺乏对VR沟通特性的系统总结及设计优化方向，需通过综述填补这一空白。
AI.method: 遵循PRISMA指南，对过去十年内关于社交VR沟通的研究进行系统性文献综述。
AI.result: 明确了当前社交VR沟通的设计实践与挑战，识别了提升沟通效果及可访问性的研究机会。
AI.conclusion: 通过总结现有研究，为社交VR应用的设计提供了理论支持与优化方向，推动其更广泛的应用。

authors_text: Jiajian Lu,Offer Grembek,Mark Hansen
title: Connecting Surrogate Safety Measures to Crash Probablity via Causal Probabilistic Time Series Prediction
title_zh: 通过因果概率时间序列预测将替代安全措施与碰撞概率联系起来
abstract: Surrogate safety measures can provide fast and pro-active safety analysis and give insights on the pre-crash process and crash failure mechanism by studying near misses. However, validating surrogate safety measures by connecting them to crashes is still an open question. This paper proposed a method to connect surrogate safety measures to crash probability using probabilistic time series prediction. The method used sequences of speed, acceleration and time-to-collision to estimate the probability density functions of those variables with transformer masked autoregressive flow (transformer-MAF). The autoregressive structure mimicked the causal relationship between condition, action and crash outcome and the probability density functions are used to calculate the conditional action probability, crash probability and conditional crash probability. The predicted sequence is accurate and the estimated probability is reasonable under both traffic conflict context and normal interaction context and the conditional crash probability shows the effectiveness of evasive action to avoid crashes in a counterfactual experiment.
summary: Surrogate safety measures can provide fast and pro-active safety analysis and give insights on the pre-crash process and crash failure mechanism by studying near misses. However, validating surrogate safety measures by connecting them to crashes is still an open question. This paper proposed a method to connect surrogate safety measures to crash probability using probabilistic time series prediction. The method used sequences of speed, acceleration and time-to-collision to estimate the probability density functions of those variables with transformer masked autoregressive flow (transformer-MAF). The autoregressive structure mimicked the causal relationship between condition, action and crash outcome and the probability density functions are used to calculate the conditional action probability, crash probability and conditional crash probability. The predicted sequence is accurate and the estimated probability is reasonable under both traffic conflict context and normal interaction context and the conditional crash probability shows the effectiveness of evasive action to avoid crashes in a counterfactual experiment.
abstract_zh: 替代安全措施可以提供快速、主动的安全分析，并通过研究未遂事件来深入了解碰撞前过程和碰撞故障机制。然而，通过将替代安全措施与事故联系起来来验证它们仍然是一个悬而未决的问题。本文提出了一种使用概率时间序列预测将替代安全措施与碰撞概率联系起来的方法。该方法使用速度、加速度和碰撞时间序列来估计这些变量的概率密度函数以及变压器掩蔽自回归流（变压器-MAF）。自回归结构模拟了条件、动作和碰撞结果之间的因果关系，并使用概率密度函数来计算条件动作概率、碰撞概率和条件碰撞概率。在交通冲突情境和正常交互情境下，预测序列准确，估计概率合理，条件碰撞概率在反事实实验中显示了规避动作避免碰撞的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通过因果概率时间序列预测将替代安全指标与碰撞概率关联的方法。
AI.motivation: 替代安全指标虽能提供预碰撞过程分析，但如何有效验证其与实际碰撞的关联性仍是未解难题。
AI.method: 基于速度、加速度和时间到碰撞序列，采用Transformer-MAF模型估计概率密度函数，通过自回归结构模拟条件-行为-碰撞结果的因果关系，计算条件行为概率与碰撞概率。
AI.result: 预测序列准确且概率估计合理，反事实实验验证了避让行为对降低碰撞概率的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法成功建立了替代安全指标与碰撞概率的因果联系，为交通安全评估提供了新工具。

The method used sequences of speed, acceleration and time-to-collision to estimate the probability density functions of those variables with transformer masked autoregressive flow (transformer-MAF). The autoregressive structure mimicked the causal relationship between condition, action and crash outcome and the probability density functions are used to calculate the conditional action probability, crash probability and conditional crash probability. The predicted sequence is accurate and the estimated probability is reasonable under both traffic conflict context and normal interaction context and the conditional crash probability shows the effectiveness of evasive action to avoid crashes in a counterfactual experiment. 替代安全措施可以提供快速、主动的安全分析，并通过研究未遂事件来深入了解碰撞前过程和碰撞故障机制。然而，通过将替代安全措施与事故联系起来来验证它们仍然是一个悬而未决的问题。本文提出了一种使用概率时间序列预测将替代安全措施与碰撞概率联系起来的方法。该方法使用速度、加速度和碰撞时间序列来估计这些变量的概率密度函数以及变压器掩蔽自回归流（变压器-MAF）。自回归结构模拟了条件、动作和碰撞结果之间的因果关系，并使用概率密度函数来计算条件动作概率、碰撞概率和条件碰撞概率。在交通冲突情境和正常交互情境下，预测序列准确，估计概率合理，条件碰撞概率在反事实实验中显示了规避动作避免碰撞的有效性。 本文提出一种通过因果概率时间序列预测将替代安全指标与碰撞概率关联的方法。 Jiajian Lu,Offer Grembek,Mark Hansen cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Marcus Friedel,Ehud Sharlin,Ryo Suzuki
title: HapticLever: Kinematic Force Feedback using a 3D Pantograph
title_zh: HapticLever：使用 3D 受电弓的运动力反馈
abstract: HapticLever is a new kinematic approach for VR haptics which uses a 3D pantograph to stiffly render large-scale surfaces using small-scale proxies. The HapticLever approach does not consume power to render forces, but rather puts a mechanical constraint on the end effector using a small-scale proxy surface. The HapticLever approach provides stiff force feedback when the user interacts with a static virtual surface, but allows the user to move their arm freely when moving through free virtual space. We present the problem space, the related work, and the HapticLever design approach.
summary: HapticLever is a new kinematic approach for VR haptics which uses a 3D pantograph to stiffly render large-scale surfaces using small-scale proxies. The HapticLever approach does not consume power to render forces, but rather puts a mechanical constraint on the end effector using a small-scale proxy surface. The HapticLever approach provides stiff force feedback when the user interacts with a static virtual surface, but allows the user to move their arm freely when moving through free virtual space. We present the problem space, the related work, and the HapticLever design approach.
abstract_zh: HapticLever 是一种新的 VR 触觉运动学方法，它使用 3D 受电弓通过小规模代理来僵硬地渲染大规模表面。 HapticLever 方法不消耗功率来渲染力，而是使用小规模代理表面对末端执行器施加机械约束。当用户与静态虚拟表面交互时，HapticLever 方法提供刚性力反馈，但允许用户在自由虚拟空间中移动时自由移动手臂。我们介绍了问题空间、相关工作和 HapticLever 设计方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出HapticLever，一种利用3D连杆机构实现大范围VR触觉反馈的新方法。
AI.motivation: 传统力反馈设备需消耗大量能量且难以实现大范围刚性表面交互，需更高效机械解决方案。
AI.method: 通过3D连杆机构将小规模代理表面与末端执行器机械耦合，利用运动学约束实现无耗能的力反馈。
AI.result: 成功构建原型系统，验证了在静态虚拟表面提供刚性反馈与自由空间无约束运动的可行性。
AI.conclusion: HapticLever为VR触觉交互提供了低功耗、大范围的机械力反馈新范式，具有重要应用潜力。

authors_text: Keita Mason,Joshua Knights,Milad Ramezani,Peyman Moghadam,Dimity Miller
title: Uncertainty-Aware Lidar Place Recognition in Novel Environments
title_zh: 新环境中的不确定性激光雷达位置识别
comment: 8 pages, 4 figures. Accepted for publication at IEEE IROS 2023
comments: 8 pages, 4 figures. Accepted for publication at IEEE IROS 2023
abstract: State-of-the-art lidar place recognition models exhibit unreliable performance when tested on environments different from their training dataset, which limits their use in complex and evolving environments. To address this issue, we investigate the task of uncertainty-aware lidar place recognition, where each predicted place must have an associated uncertainty that can be used to identify and reject incorrect predictions. We introduce a novel evaluation protocol and present the first comprehensive benchmark for this task, testing across five uncertainty estimation techniques and three large-scale datasets. Our results show that an Ensembles approach is the highest performing technique, consistently improving the performance of lidar place recognition and uncertainty estimation in novel environments, though it incurs a computational cost. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/csiro-robotics/Uncertainty-LPR.
summary: State-of-the-art lidar place recognition models exhibit unreliable performance when tested on environments different from their training dataset, which limits their use in complex and evolving environments. To address this issue, we investigate the task of uncertainty-aware lidar place recognition, where each predicted place must have an associated uncertainty that can be used to identify and reject incorrect predictions. We introduce a novel evaluation protocol and present the first comprehensive benchmark for this task, testing across five uncertainty estimation techniques and three large-scale datasets. Our results show that an Ensembles approach is the highest performing technique, consistently improving the performance of lidar place recognition and uncertainty estimation in novel environments, though it incurs a computational cost. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/csiro-robotics/Uncertainty-LPR.
abstract_zh: 最先进的激光雷达地点识别模型在不同于训练数据集的环境中进行测试时表现出不可靠的性能，这限制了它们在复杂和不断变化的环境中的使用。为了解决这个问题，我们研究了不确定性感知激光雷达位置识别的任务，其中每个预测的位置必须具有相关的不确定性，可用于识别和拒绝错误的预测。我们引入了一种新颖的评估协议，并提出了该任务的第一个综合基准，测试了五种不确定性估计技术和三个大型数据集。我们的结果表明，集成方法是性能最高的技术，可以持续提高新环境中激光雷达位置识别和不确定性估计的性能，尽管它会产生计算成本。代码可在 https://github.com/csiro-robotics/Uncertainty-LPR 上公开获取。

AI.tldr: 提出一种在新环境中具有不确定性的激光雷达地点识别方法
AI.motivation: 现有激光雷达地点识别模型在训练数据不同的新环境中表现不可靠，限制其在复杂动态环境中的应用，需提升可靠性
AI.method: 引入新的评估协议，构建首个综合基准测试，评估五种不确定性估计技术在三个大规模数据集上的表现
AI.result: 集成方法在性能上最优，显著提升新环境中的识别与不确定性估计效果，但计算成本较高
AI.conclusion: 集成方法在性能上最优但计算成本高，需进一步研究平衡性能与效率的方法
